scenario: Beacon Telco runs Camunda 8.8 SaaS for 9000 instances of a subscriber onboarding process. After a model defect was discovered, 400 instances are stuck at the wrong service task, and the operations team needs to relocate all of them to a different element inside the same process model in one action without redeploying or losing their variable state.
question: Which Operate feature lets operators move many active process instances to a different element in one action?

options.text: The Resume from incident operation moves a batch of active process instances forward to a different element of the same model
The Cancel and recreate operation moves a batch of active process instances forward to a different element of the same model
The Batch move modification moves a batch of active process instances forward to a different element of the same model
The Delete and replay operation moves a batch of active process instances forward to a different element of the same model

a.text: Incorrect. Resume from incident is not a documented Operate operation; incidents are resolved by updating variables and retrying the task, not by jumping a batch of instances to a different element.

b.text: Incorrect. Cancelling and recreating would drop history and start fresh instances; the documented batch move action keeps the existing instances and relocates their tokens in place.

c.text: Correct. Per docs, Operate offers a batch move action that relocates a set of active process instances to a different element of the same process so execution can continue from there.

d.text: Incorrect. Operate can delete finished instances, but Delete and replay is not a documented action for relocating active instances to a different element of the same process model.

explanation: The Operate docs list batch move as one of its key capabilities on active process instances, where many instances are moved to a different element of the same process so execution can continue from the new element.
